manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm up at five every day, no exceptions. You don't get anywhere only training on the days you feel like it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's so disciplined, up before dawn every single day. After dating guys who couldn't hold a routine, it's honestly attractive.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Discipline stated as moral law (no exceptions); right now only his. The absoluteness is the faint seam. Admirable cover.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't do lazy weekends. There's always something to maintain, train, or fix.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's always productive, always improving something. It makes my slow Sundays feel a little aimless by comparison, but I admire it.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Contempt for rest folded into work ethic (idle hands); her downtime already reframed by her as aimless. Empathy1 floor under the discipline.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Everything in my place has a spot and goes back to it. Keeps my head straight.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: His apartment is immaculate and everything has its place. He's so put-together. I felt embarrassed about my own clutter.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Rigidity (Cog-flex2) presented as mental-health practice; she measures her home against his standard and finds it short. The standard entering the room.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Put your card away. When you're with me, I take care of it. That's the man's job, simple as that.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He always pays, always. He says it's the man's job. It's old-fashioned but after splitting every bill with exes it feels like being taken care of.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Provision tied to role/order (the man's job, simple as that); gendered hierarchy stated as settled fact, not romance. Reads as chivalry, functions as position.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I've got a system for the money, savings, the works. You won't ever have to stress about it. I'll handle that side.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's completely on top of his finances and says I'll never have to worry about money with him. It's a huge relief honestly.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Provision that removes her from a domain (that side is his); care and the architecture of control are the same act. Distinct from Marley (ownership)/Morgan (debt): this is role.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I already booked it, paid, sorted the whole trip. You don't need to do anything but show up. I prefer it that way.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He planned and paid for our entire trip without me lifting a finger. He likes handling everything. It's a different world from what I'm used to.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: I prefer it that way' is the tell: provision structured so decisions route through him. She's a guest on a trip about her. Generous on its face.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: A man who can't provide isn't really a man, in my opinion. I was raised to take care of what's mine. That's not negotiable.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He has strong views about a man's duty to provide, and he lives by them. I find his sense of responsibility really reassuring.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Provision as non-negotiable identity (not negotiable, what's mine); gendered order plus a faint possessive, wrapped in duty.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Nobody handed me anything. Everything I have, I built myself. That matters to me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's completely self-made, built everything from nothing. There's something really impressive about that kind of drive.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Self-made narrative as moral identity; 'no excuses, no handouts' is the merit-doctrine that later sorts people and her. Work ethic real, yardstick forming.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't respect much, but I respect someone who earns their place. Can't stand people who expect things they didn't work for.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He has so much respect for hard work and zero patience for people who coast. I like that he has standards.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Merit as the basis of who's worthy (earns their place); contempt for the 'undeserving' (Disrespect9) stated as principle. She codes contempt as standards.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I set a goal, I hit it. Made rank ahead of schedule, hit every number I set. That's just discipline applied over time.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's accomplished so much and so young, and he's modest about it, says it's just discipline. I'm proud to be with someone like that.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Achievement as proof of earned rank (Grandiosity8, earned-not-fragile vs Evan); 'made rank' anchors the institutional hierarchy. She's placed near the top of his order.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't quit. Ever. On anything, or anyone. Once I'm committed, that's it, I'm in for good.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He told me he never quits on anyone he's committed to. After being ghosted and let down, hearing that made me feel so secure.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Persistence (Goal-persistence8) as devotion; reassuring. The seam: 'never quits' also means never yields, never lets her leave a thing alone. Invisible now.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: When something's not up to standard, I don't let it slide. I fix it. You don't get anywhere accepting good enough.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He doesn't let things slide, he fixes whatever isn't right. I've always been more of a 'good enough' person, so it's kind of inspiring.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Doesn't let it slide' on standards; now tasks, but the engine that won't let her deviations slide either. She positions herself as the lesser standard.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'll out-stubborn any problem. I just don't stop. Most people fold when it gets hard. I'm not most people.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's relentless, he just doesn't give up on anything. It's the opposite of every flaky person I've dated and I love it.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Grit fused with 'I'm not most people' (merit-superiority); immovability as strength. Cold persistence, contrast with Marley's volatility.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: If we ever had a problem, I wouldn't walk. I'd dig in until it's solved. I don't do unfinished.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He says he'd never walk away from a problem between us, he'd stay and work it. That kind of commitment is exactly what I've wanted.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Reassurance carries a slip toward 'solved my [way]' he corrects; persistence as never-conceding. She hears only the staying. Pattern: his way is the only finish line.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Everyone's got their place, and things run right when people stay in it. That's just order.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He believes everyone has their place and society works when people respect that. I'd never thought about it that way but he makes it sound sensible.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: The authoritarian core stated plainly (everyone's got a place, stay in it); hierarchy reframed as benign order. She's being introduced to the frame she'll be placed in.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I've got no patience for people who think the rules don't apply to them.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He has real respect for the way things are supposed to be done. It's grounding, like he has a code, after dating men with none.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Conventionalism (a way things are done, a reason) plus intolerance of deviation; 'rules don't apply to them' marks the cardinal sin as questioning the order. She reads it as 'a code.'

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm not saying everyone's equal. People earn their standing or they don't.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's pretty unsentimental about people being where they've earned to be. Part of me flinched, but he lives by it himself, so it's hard to argue.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Merit-justified hierarchy (earn their standing); 'gets soft' is the contempt. Her flinch immediately overridden by his self-application. The just-world alibi for ranking.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Your father's a serious man. You can tell he built something real. I made sure to show him the respect he's owed.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He was so respectful with my dad, almost formal, and my parents adored him instantly. It meant a lot that he took it seriously.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Eager deference upward (respect he's owed) reads as good character; the same ranking instinct that judges downward. Charms the authority figures. Cover for the structure.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You respect the position even if you don't like the man in it. That's what holds everything together.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He has this old-school respect for authority and rank. It's a little intense but it comes from somewhere principled, the military I think.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Submission to established authority as load-bearing belief (respect the position, chain of command); institutional grounding. 'A little intense' is the seam she sources to principle.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I always make a point with the boss, the brass, whoever's above me. You handle yourself right with the people who matter, doors open.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He knows exactly how to carry himself around important people. Watching him work a room of higher-ups, I felt proud to be on his arm.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Strategic deference to 'people who matter' (ranked by power) presented as savvy; she sees polish, not the rank-calculus. Sets up the downward contrast.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Look at that guy, no bearing, lets himself go. You can read a man off how he carries himself.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's quick to judge people who seem undisciplined. It can be a little harsh, but he holds himself to the same bar, so I let it go.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Contempt downward (Disrespect9) moralized by the shared merit-bar; 'reads character off bearing' is the sorting instinct. She excuses via his self-application.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Your friend who job-hops and is always broke? Nice enough, but she's going nowhere. Be careful who you take after.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's not impressed by some of my friends, thinks they lack direction. He might have a point, but it stung to hear about people I love.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Isolation9 via merit-ranking, not jealousy: her friends sorted as low-status and a contaminating influence (watch how much you take after). Behavior-steer away from them. The sting registered, half-conceded.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't buy the whole 'they're struggling' thing. Everyone's got it hard. Some people get up anyway.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He doesn't have much sympathy for people going through a rough patch, says everyone struggles. It's the one place his toughness feels a little cold to me.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Empathy1 bare: struggle recoded as weakness/excuse. She names a flicker of coldness here (closest she comes) but does not generalize it. Just-world floor.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: That's not how you do that. Here, watch. There's a correct way and a sloppy way, and the correct way takes the same time.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He showed me a 'better' way to do something I've done fine for years. He wasn't unkind about it, and honestly his way was more efficient.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Unsolicited correction toward 'the correct way' (Cog-flex2); her competent method recoded as sloppy. Infantilization via instruction. She concedes the efficiency, not the overreach.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: We're going to get you on a real routine. Up at a set time, gym, meals planned. I'll set it up. You'll feel a hundred times better.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's helping me build better habits, set me up with a whole routine. It's a lot but he's right that I've let things slip. I want to be better.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Her life restructured to his standard, framed as help (I'll set it up); the recruit-being-trained dynamic. Behavior-steer, one-directional. She frames her old life as 'slipped' and aspires to his bar.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I hear you, but no. I've thought about this more than you have and I'm telling you the right way to handle it. Trust me on this.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: When we disagree he's very sure he knows best, and usually he has thought it through more. I've started just going with his judgment.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Charlot direct hit: disregards her reasoning when it disagrees with his. 'Thought about it more than you' asserts rank over her judgment. She defers, deskilling herself.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Good. That's how it's done. See, you're capable when you apply yourself, you just needed pushing.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: When I do something to his standard he gives this approval that, I won't lie, feels really good to earn. Like I passed a test.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Cold approval-of-conduct (not warmth, not the cut 'good girl') conditioned on meeting his bar; 'needed someone to hold the standard' is the infantilizing claim. She's training toward his approval (earn, graduated).

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: No reason to keep things separate if we're serious. Your schedule, your money, your goals, let's combine it. My system works.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants to merge everything fast, finances, calendars, plans. It moved quick but it felt like he was really committed, all in.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Enmeshment8 as seriousness; 'run it all as one system, mine works' is the absorption (his structure becomes theirs). Reads as commitment. The one-directionality is the tell.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: We do it this way now. I've already figured out what works, so why reinvent it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He calls his routines 'our way' now, which is sweet, though it's basically his way that I've adopted. It does work better, so I don't mind.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Our way' that is his way (Enmeshment8 + Cog-flex2); 'I'm not rigid' is the disavowal-tell. She notices the asymmetry ('basically his way') and minimizes it.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't really believe in separate lives inside a relationship. Separate accounts, separate friends, separate plans, that's how people drift.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He thinks couples shouldn't keep separate lives, that it leads to drifting apart. It makes sense, but I've noticed I see my own friends less.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Isolation9 via anti-separateness doctrine (separate friends leads to drift); merges to eliminate her independent sphere. Behavior already shifting (friends less). She supplies the logic herself.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: her-place (a good woman accepts her place)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I take care of the big picture, you make a good home and back me up. A man and a woman who both know their role, that's unbeatable.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He has clear ideas about roles, him leading, me supporting. It's traditional but there's something restful about both knowing where we stand.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Fixed gendered place stated as functional order (know their role); her sphere defined as support. 'Restful' is how she experiences being slotted. Conventionalism (Unorthodoxy1).

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't mind you having an opinion. I mind the questioning, especially in front of people.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He distinguishes between sharing an opinion and 'undermining' him, especially in public. I've learned to pick my battles.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: The cardinal sin named: questioning the authority itself (not being wrong) is the offense. 'Undermining in front of people' polices her in public. Behavior-steer toward deference. Distinct from Avery (who'd argue she's WRONG).

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Someone has to have the final call, and that's me. Things fall apart otherwise.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He believes someone needs the final say and that it's him. He frames it as just structure, the way a team needs a captain, and the way he says it, it sounds almost reasonable.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Final-authority claim laundered as 'structure, not ego' (the disavowal-tell); submission expected on principle. 'Almost reasonable' is the seam she talks past. Dominance10.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm not angry. I'm disappointed you handled it that way after everything we talked about.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: When I don't meet his standard he gets this quiet, disappointed thing, worse than yelling honestly. I end up working hard to get back in his good graces.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Shaming-as-discipline (typology) in the COLD register (Dysregulation3, not Marley's heat): disappointment as the punishment, 'expected more discipline from you' infantilizes. She does repair labor. No blowup, no incident.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Everyone at the congregation is going to love you. They're good people, real community, they look out for their own. You'll have a whole family there.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: His community welcomed me so warmly, it's this tight, caring group. After feeling a bit adrift, suddenly having a whole built-in family is wonderful.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Isolation9 via ABSORPTION not jealousy: she's folded into his total community (a whole family there). The warm welcome disguises the replacement of her own world. Belonging is the hook.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: community (the order is bigger than him)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: It's not just my opinion on how to live. It's something bigger than me. I answer to it too. That's what keeps a person honest.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's so grounded in his faith and answers to something bigger than himself. There's a humility in it that I really respect.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: His authority laundered through a higher order (I answer to it too); submission-upward made concrete and made to look like humility. His standards now read as the order's, not his preference.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: We don't really do that in our circle. It's not a rule I made up, it's just how things are done where I come from. You'll get the rhythm of it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: There are certain ways things are done in his community and I'm learning them. It's not like he's making me, it's just their culture, and I want to fit in.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Control depersonalized into community norm (not a rule I made up); much harder to push back on than a preference. She pre-emptively clears him ('not him being controlling') which is the disavowal-tell.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: The way we believe it, a husband leads and a wife supports that, and he's accountable for the whole household. It's not about less-than. It's about order and who answers for what.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He has sincere beliefs about a husband leading and a wife supporting, from his faith. It's traditional, but he frames it as responsibility falling on him, and there's something steadying about it.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Gendered headship as devout belief (we believe), pre-empting the 'less-than' objection; framed as his burden of accountability. Early-stage: stated as sincere worldview she finds steadying, NOT yet enforced on her. Conventionalism (Unorthodoxy1).

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Just so you know how it works here, people notice things, they talk. It's a close community. Reflects on both of us how we carry ourselves.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He mentioned the community's pretty close-knit and people notice things. I hadn't thought about being watched, but I get wanting to make a good impression.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Enforcement distributed to the community (people notice, they talk) so control is deniable and not-him; 'reflects on both of us' begins making her conduct accountable to the structure. The surveillance seam, kept light.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: We leave at seven. Not seven-oh-five. On time is late in my book, early is on time. Says everything about a person, honestly.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's militantly punctual and I've had to step it up. He's right that I was always a bit late. It's actually made me more reliable.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Time as a moral test (on time is late); punctuality standard imposed and moralized (says everything about a person). She adopts it and credits him with improving her. Behavioral micromanagement, on-fingerprint.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: What's the plan for your day? Walk me through it. I just like to know the schedule, keeps us both efficient and on the same page.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He likes to know my schedule for the day, which felt like a lot at first, but he says it keeps us coordinated and honestly it does.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Her time made accountable to him (walk me through it) under an efficiency frame; the accounting of her hours is the behavioral control. She registers 'a lot' then accepts the rationale. Distinct from Marley's possessive checking (this is logistics/order).

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: What did you actually get done today? A day with nothing to show for it is a day you don't get back. I can't sit still like that.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He asks what I accomplished each day. It can feel like a performance review, but it does push me to use my time better.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Idle time recoded as moral failure (nothing to show for it); her day subject to his audit (performance review, in her own words). Empathy1 floor (rest = waste). She frames the audit as motivating.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I run a tight schedule and it works, so we'll build yours around the fixed points in mine. Easier than two systems fighting each other.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: We've organized my week around his fixed commitments, since his schedule is less flexible. It made sense and my time feels more structured now.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Her schedule subordinated to his as the fixed reference (build yours around mine); Enmeshment8 + the one-directional standard. 'Made sense' is the rationale she supplies. The asymmetry invisible to her.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Wear something on the conservative side tonight, these are serious people. How you present reflects on me, and on us as a couple. Presentation matters.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He asked me to dress more conservatively for an event with important people, said it reflects on us. It's not how I'd usually dress but I want to make him proud.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Appearance policed as REPRESENTATION of him/the order (reflects on me, on us), not jealousy (Marley) or appraisal (Evan). Behavior-steer toward his standard of appropriate. She complies to 'make him proud.' Charlot: tries to change you.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: When we're out with my people, keep it composed. No getting loud or too opinionated. There's a way we conduct ourselves. It matters more than you'd think.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He likes me to be composed around his community, not too loud or forward. I've toned myself down at those things. I figure every group has its norms.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Public conduct policed as conformity to the order (a way we conduct ourselves); her self-expression (loud, opinionated) recoded as inappropriate. She tones herself down, naming it as norms. Self-erasure framed as etiquette.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: You represent me when you're out there, whether I'm with you or not. I hold myself to a standard and I need the people closest to me to hold it too.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He said I represent him even when he's not there, which is a big responsibility but also kind of an honor, like he trusts me with his name.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Her autonomy recoded as representation of him (you represent me); his standard extended to her conduct even in his absence (remote behavioral control). She reframes the burden as honor/trust. Grandiosity8.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: That story you told at dinner, maybe keep that one in the vault going forward. Not our crowd's kind of humor. I'm looking out for how you come across.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He gently told me a joke I made didn't land with his crowd and to skip it next time. A little embarrassing, but he's just helping me read the room.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Post-hoc correction of her self-expression to fit the order (not our crowd's humor), framed as protective coaching (looking out for how you come across). She absorbs it as her social error. Infantilizing.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: It's not me telling you what to do. It's just that statistically that route's more dangerous at night, so we don't take it. It's not personal, it's facts.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He always has a logical reason for the things he'd prefer I do or not do, usually safety. It's hard to argue with because he's usually right.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Control delivered as impersonal reason (it's not me, it's facts) so non-compliance feels irrational; the justification is the mechanism (Cog-flex2/Sense-of-Self10). 'Hard to argue with' = the design. NOT winning an argument (Avery): he treats her compliance as the only rational option.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: If you understood the reasoning the way I do, you'd land where I have. So let me walk you through why, and then it'll make sense.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: When I disagree he patiently explains his reasoning until I see it his way. He's not dismissive, he genuinely thinks I'll agree once I get it. And often I do.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Her disagreement recoded as incomplete understanding (if you understood, you'd agree); he doesn't argue the point (Avery) he re-explains the standard until she yields. Charlot: disregards your reasoning. The patience is the trap.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm not being inflexible. I just have principles, and a principle you abandon when it's inconvenient was never a principle. You'll understand that about me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's very principled and doesn't bend his rules even when I'd prefer he did. I used to think that was stubborn but now I kind of admire the consistency.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Rigidity (Cog-flex2) reframed as integrity (principle you abandon was never a principle); her preferences subordinated to his fixed code. She converts 'stubborn' into 'admirable consistency.' The immovability seam.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I've got rules I live by and I don't break them, not for convenience, not for anybody. That's just who I am.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He lives by this clear set of personal rules and never bends them. After dating guys with no backbone, a man with a real code is so attractive.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Rule applied to HIMSELF (rules I live by); reads as integrity/backbone. The seam is the absoluteness (not for anybody, never bend) that will later extend to her. Early, glossy, self-directed. She's happy.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: There's a right way to do most things. I didn't invent it, it's just how it's done properly. I follow it because it works.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He believes there's a proper way to do things and he follows it to the letter. It's part of why he's so successful and reliable, I think.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Conventionalism (a right way, how it's done properly) as impersonal fact he submits to (didn't invent it); attributed by her to his success/reliability. The non-negotiable standard, not yet aimed at her.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: its-the-rules (follows the rules at work, respected)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I go by the book at work. Every procedure, every standard, no cutting corners. It's why they trust me with the big stuff.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's so respected at his job because he does everything by the book, never cuts a corner. Watching how seriously people take him is impressive.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Rule-following as the source of his standing/respect (why they trust me); she admires the success it produces. Self-applied discipline, fully early-stage, reads as professional excellence.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I hold myself to the rules harder than I'd ever hold anyone else. If I cheat on the small stuff, the whole thing falls apart.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He's actually strictest with himself, holds himself to a higher bar than anyone. That kind of self-discipline is rare and I really respect it.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Self-applied strictness (strictest with myself) is the move that LICENSES later imposition on her (if I hold myself to it...); reads now as admirable rigor. She respects it; the imposition seam is invisible.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Rules aren't there to box you in. They're there so things don't fall into chaos. People who roll their eyes at structure are usually the ones whose lives are a mess.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He has this grounded view that rules and structure are what keep life from falling apart. He has a point, the most chaotic people I know do reject all that.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Conventionalism defended as anti-chaos (rules so things don't fall into chaos); the faint contempt downward (people whose lives are a mess) laundered as observation. She agrees, adopting the frame. Apple of order.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: its-the-rules (the rule is the authority, not him)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: Don't go by what I want, go by what's correct. I'm not the boss of it either, the standard is the standard. I just keep to it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He insists it's not about him being in charge, it's about doing what's correct. He frames himself as serving the standard, not bossing people. It's humble in a way.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Authority displaced onto 'the standard' (I'm not the boss of it either) so compliance isn't to HIM, it's to correctness; this is the laundering that later makes resisting him feel like resisting truth. She reads humility. Sets up rules-with-reasons.

manipulation_vocabulary.mechanism: its-the-rules (faint first inclusion of her, gentle)
manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: When you're with someone like me, there's a rhythm to how things run. Nothing heavy, you'll pick it up. It's a good way to live, you'll see.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He says being with him comes with a certain rhythm to things and that I'll pick it up. It sounds nice honestly, like a more orderly life than I've had.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: The FIRST faint extension of the rules toward her (a rhythm to how things run, you'll pick it up) kept soft and appealing (nothing heavy, a good way to live); behavior-steer in seed form. Early-stage: an invitation, not a command. She's drawn to the order.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm the provider type. I want to be the one taking care of things so you don't have to worry.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He sounds capable and steady, like I could finally stop carrying everything.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Leadership becomes authority over her, not just competence in his own life.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I have high standards for how a household runs, and I don't apologize for that.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It feels reassuring, like he can bring order to chaos.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Done right means done his way; her methods become mistakes to correct.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I believe the man should carry the weight in a relationship. That's how I was raised.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds protective and generous, like I wouldn't be alone anymore.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Handling everything centralizes power; her practical independence can erode.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I make the hard calls so the people I love don't have to. That's what a man does.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He sounds confident, like he won't leave me stuck in uncertainty.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Decisiveness can erase consultation; once he decides, her input becomes delay.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I like a household that runs on a system. Everyone knows their role and things just work.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It feels stable, like he thinks ahead and won't drift.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Planning becomes possession of the future; her changes or preferences become disruptions.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm old-fashioned about who does what, and honestly most women find it a relief.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds safe, like he'll prevent problems before they happen.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Not leaving things to chance can become surveillance and restriction.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: When I commit to a woman, I take full responsibility for her. All of it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds mature and dependable, like he takes his role seriously.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Responsibility may mean gendered hierarchy where he leads and she complies.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't think a relationship is equal so much as it's two people with their own lanes.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He sounds strong and trustworthy in a crisis.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Crisis competence becomes justification for taking over outside crises too.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm not controlling, I just have a clear idea of how things should be done, and I'm usually right.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds honest and maybe even endearing.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Particular becomes a softened word for rigid control over shared life.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I set the direction and I expect to be trusted on it. That's earned, but I expect it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It feels impressive, like he can guide us well.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He will privilege his judgment over hers under the banner of efficiency.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want to build something solid, and that means someone who'll follow my lead on the big things.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds caring, like he builds stability for others.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Structure can become a cage, especially when safety is defined by him.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I look after my own. In exchange I ask for loyalty and that my judgment gets respected.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He sounds dependable, like he won't make me do everything.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Stepping up can mean stepping over her decisions.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I think a good man should make his partner's life simpler by making the decisions.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It feels like relief, especially after doing everything myself.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Making life easier may mean removing choice and making dependency feel comfortable.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I run a tight ship at home, finances, schedule, all of it, and it works.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He sounds intentional, like he knows himself and his values.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: His life run becomes the template she must adapt to.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I don't do chaos. I need a partner who's comfortable letting me steer.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It feels reassuring that he won't be flaky.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Firm plans can become inflexible demands where her uncertainty is not allowed.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm protective to a fault. I need to know where things stand and where my partner is.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds attentive and invested.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Hands-on becomes intrusive when she becomes something important to manage.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I believe respect in a relationship means deferring to whoever's got the better judgment. Usually that's me.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He sounds capable, like he won't let things spiral.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Immediate solutions can bypass her feelings, consent, and process.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I've built a stable life and I want a woman who appreciates being taken care of within it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds warm and intentional, like he cares about domestic life.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: His standards become household rules she must satisfy.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'll handle the world so you don't have to, but my house runs my way.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds brave, like he'll take pressure off me.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He authorizes harshness as protection and appoints himself moral enforcer.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm the kind of man who'll fix everything for you, as long as you trust how I do it.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: It sounds romantic, like surrendering control would create ease.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: Full trust becomes compliance; questioning him becomes failure to support his leadership.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm looking for someone traditional who puts family first.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: We have the same old-school values, family matters to me too, so it feels aligned.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is pre-screening for deference to male authority and a hierarchy he leads, ensuring she accepts his control.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a partner who lets me take the lead.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He is a confident, protective man who wants to take care of things, which is hot.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is directly selecting for submissiveness, ensuring his decisions will never be questioned or countermanded.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I like a woman who values a man who provides.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants to be a traditional provider and protector, which makes me feel safe.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is using financial provision as a tool to demand total ownership and control over her choices.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a partner who respects how a relationship should be run.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He appreciates traditional gender roles and clarity about who does what.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is masking his demand for absolute structural dominance behind the benign language of natural roles.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a feminine woman who's happy in a traditional role.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He loves true femininity and wants a classic chivalrous relationship.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is filtering for a target who believes compliance and deference are essential components of her womanhood.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm looking for a girl who trusts her man's judgment completely.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants a deep trusting bond where he feels respected as the leader.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for a blind obedience that will allow him to make major life decisions without her consent.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I like a woman who focuses on creating a peaceful home.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants a beautiful calm domestic life where we can both relax.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is assigning her the total responsibility for domestic labor while keeping all structural power for himself.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I need someone who understands that the man protects the household.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He is fiercely protective and wants to make sure I am always safe.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is framing his hyper-surveillance, jealousy, and restriction of her movement as chivalrous protection.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a sweet family-oriented woman who wants to stay home with kids.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants a beautiful traditional family and values motherhood,  which is wonderful.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is seeking to cut off her financial independence and career ties, making it incredibly difficult for her to leave.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I like a partner who doesn't challenge my authority in front of others.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He values public respect and wants us to look like a united front.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is ensuring she will never look for outside help or expose his controlling behavior to friends.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a woman who takes pride in taking care of her family.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He appreciates hard work and traditional domestic devotion, which feels rewarding.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is looking for a target who will accept a subservient position as her primary moral duty.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a lady who knows how to carry herself with modesty.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He respects me and wants a classy woman, not someone desperate for attention.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is starting the process of controlling her wardrobe, makeup, and self-expression under the guise of modesty.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I'm looking for a partner who is comfortable with a strong old-school provider.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He is a real man's man who wants to take care of his woman completely.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is screening out independent women who would demand financial transparency and equal partnership.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I like a woman who lets a man be a man.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He values his masculinity and wants a relationship where he can be strong.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is establishing a pass for aggressive, entitled, or patriarchal behavior as just being a man.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I need someone who defers to my experience in big decisions.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He is mature and experienced, so it makes sense that he wants to guide us.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is demand-testing for a target who will surrender her financial and geographic autonomy.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a traditional marriage built on clear distinct roles.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He likes structure and clarity, which makes a relationship feel stable.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is establishing an authoritarian framework where his role is command and her role is obedience.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want a woman who values family and where she comes from.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He is high-principled and wants to build a respectable family name with me.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is preparing to use family pressure and reputational guilt to keep her from breaking the marriage.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I like a girl who looks up to her partner.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He wants to be admired and respected by the woman he loves.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is selecting for an unbalance of power where she views herself as inherently inferior to him.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I need a partner who lets me handle the heavy lifting in life.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He is supportive and wants to protect me from stress and financial worry.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is attempting to take over all financial levers, leaving her without any resources of her own.

manipulation_vocabulary.his_language: I want someone who believes the husband is the head of the home.
manipulation_vocabulary.her_experience: He has strong faith and values traditional spiritual leadership, which I find grounding.
manipulation_vocabulary.expert_notices: He is securing structural justification for domestic tyranny and total control.

community_vocabulary.snippet: Where did you go to school?
community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking which college specifically — HBCU signals one path PWI signals another
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Howard signals DC elite often political; Spelman signals Black women's elite; Morehouse signals Black male leadership; Hampton signals Virginia tidewater elite; FAMU signals Florida HBCU pride

community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking whether you joined a Divine Nine sorority or fraternity
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Greek membership signals HBCU experience and middle-class trajectory and lifetime Black professional network

community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking when and where you joined your fraternity or sorority
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Crossing date and chapter signal specific line and community

community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking which specific chapter of your fraternity or sorority
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Specific chapter signals geographic and class community

community_vocabulary.snippet: Are you in Jack and Jill?
community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking whether you're a member of the elite Black children's social organization
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Signals middle-to-upper-middle-class parenting and multigenerational Black professional class

community_vocabulary.snippet: What chapter of Jack and Jill?
community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking which specific Jack and Jill chapter
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Specific chapter signals geographic and class community

community_vocabulary.translation: Asked of women — he's checking invitation-only Black women's service organization
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Major top-tier Black professional women's signal

community_vocabulary.snippet: Is your husband in the Boulé?
community_vocabulary.translation: Asked of married women — checking invitation-only top Black professional men's fraternity
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Major top-tier Black professional men's signal

community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking whether you have a vacation home and where
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Significant resources signal and specific tradition

community_vocabulary.snippet: Do y'all go to the Vineyard?
community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking whether you go to Martha's Vineyard specifically Oak Bluffs Inkwell Beach area

community_vocabulary.snippet: Do you have a place up top?
community_vocabulary.translation: Up top' means Martha's Vineyard — he's asking if you own property there
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Owning at the Vineyard signals significant wealth and 'old' standing in Black professional circles

community_vocabulary.snippet: Where did your parents go to school?
community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking about multigenerational education
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Multigenerational HBCU signals legacy; first-generation college signals upward mobility

community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking your father's specific profession — class signal
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Doctor lawyer judge professor business owner executive signal different things

community_vocabulary.snippet: Where do your kids go to school?
community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking about your children's education
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Private school public magnet or HBCU pipeline signals class trajectory

community_vocabulary.translation: He's asking about participation in formal Black-elite social rituals
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Debutante participation signals Black social-elite engagement

community_vocabulary.translation: Specific HBCU in DC — major Black elite institution
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Signals DC elite often political-class trajectory

community_vocabulary.translation: Specific HBCU — Black women's elite institution
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Signals Black women's elite formation often professional-class

community_vocabulary.translation: Specific HBCU — Black male leadership tradition
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Signals Black male leadership formation often professional-class

community_vocabulary.translation: Specific HBCU — Virginia tidewater elite
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Signals tradition and tidewater Black elite

community_vocabulary.translation: Specific HBCU — Florida HBCU pride
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Signals Florida HBCU formation and pride

community_vocabulary.translation: The Atlanta HBCU complex (Morehouse Spelman Clark Atlanta)
Specific HBCU with engineering and aviation legacy
Specific HBCU — Catholic and medical-pipeline reputation
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Signals specific HBCU heritage often pre-med

community_vocabulary.translation: Oldest Black sorority — Divine Nine
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Carries stereotype (which insiders use as shorthand though members contest it) of respectability tradition middle-class background and historically lighter skin

community_vocabulary.translation: Divine Nine sorority known for activism orientation
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Carries reputation for civil rights tradition and broader skin-tone diversity

community_vocabulary.translation: Divine Nine sorority
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Carries reputation as more accessible and less stereotyped as elite

community_vocabulary.translation: Divine Nine sorority
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Carries reputation as more accessible less elitist reputation

community_vocabulary.translation: Oldest Black fraternity — Divine Nine
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Signals leadership and political tradition; MLK was Alpha

community_vocabulary.translation: Divine Nine fraternity
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Carries reputation for style and aesthetic culture

community_vocabulary.translation: Divine Nine fraternity
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Carries reputation for rougher masculinity tradition

community_vocabulary.translation: Divine Nine fraternity
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Carries reputation as more accessible less elite-coded

community_vocabulary.translation: The collective name for the nine historic Black Greek-letter organizations
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Standard in-group term for Black Greek life

community_vocabulary.translation: The group of people who pledged together
Lifetime sisters and brothers from pledge process
Members who pledged before you / after you
He joined his fraternity or sorority specifically in Spring 2010

Specific Black women's professional service organization

community_vocabulary.translation: Historic Black beach at Oak Bluffs Martha's Vineyard
Historic Black summer community on Long Island NY
Historic Black resort in Maryland
Historic Black resort in Michigan
Historic Black beach community in Florida

community_vocabulary.snippet: The Polar Bears (Oak Bluffs morning swimmers)
community_vocabulary.translation: Specific Oak Bluffs morning swimming group
Black professional family vacation property
Specifically inherited Black professional family vacation property
Quaker private school in DC — Obama daughters attended
Manhattan elite private school
Manhattan elite girls school
Manhattan elite school
Manhattan elite girls school
Bay Area elite Black-affirming private school

community_vocabulary.status_signal: Black professional wealth signal in LA

community_vocabulary.translation: Historic Black DC neighborhood
Historic Black DC neighborhood
Historic Black Atlanta wealthy neighborhood
Historic Black Chicago middle-class neighborhood
Multigenerational Black wealth and Vineyard or specific resort traditions
Specifically at the Vineyard or another historic Black resort
Both-parent professional families signal multigenerational middle-class stability
Specific cultural and educational formation

community_vocabulary.snippet: He went to one of the good Black schools
community_vocabulary.translation: Specifically elite HBCU not just any Black school
She went to or her kids go to Sidwell Friends
He went to or his kids go to Dalton
She's one of few Black students or professionals in elite space

community_vocabulary.snippet: He's the only one in the C-suite
community_vocabulary.translation: He's one of few Black executives

community_vocabulary.snippet: He had the talk with the kids
community_vocabulary.translation: He had the specific conversation about police interaction with his children
Effort required to access opportunity as a Black person
In-group acknowledgment of shared racial experience

community_vocabulary.snippet: I had to code-switch all day
community_vocabulary.translation: Performed cultural labor of shifting linguistic registers
She's an authentic Black community member
He doesn't represent or understand Black community
community_vocabulary.notes: Watch who he applies this to

community_vocabulary.translation: Same as he's not for us — lacks Black-cultural fluency
Going to Martha's Vineyard specifically
Family vacationed at the Vineyard
She participated in a formal debutante ball — usually Links cotillion or similar
Lighter skin color — operates as social shorthand in some Black professional circles
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Carries class-and-status implications in some circles which is harmful but operating

community_vocabulary.translation: Darker skin color — operates as social shorthand
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Carries different class-and-status implications which is harmful but operating

community_vocabulary.snippet: She's pretty for a dark-skinned girl
community_vocabulary.translation: Direct colorist statement — qualifies beauty by skin tone
Hair texture perceived as closer to non-Black hair — operates as colorist shorthand
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Colorism and texturism marker which is harmful but operating

community_vocabulary.translation: Unrelaxed unprocessed hair
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Often signals newer pride orientation or specific generation

community_vocabulary.translation: Chemically straightened hair
community_vocabulary.status_signal: Often signals older respectability tradition or specific community expectations

community_vocabulary.translation: Specific natural hair style
Cutting off relaxed hair to transition to natural
In-group acknowledgment of shared racial experience without naming it
